objective: Implement Jitsi-aware agent meeting participation for Krate dispatch runs: AgentStack meeting capability, meetingRef dispatch, Jitsi sidecar Job manifest, in-meeting MCP tools, auto-dispatch from meetings, and web UI surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Jitsi-capable stacks can declare meeting capability, provider reference, and role/capability config with validation.
Manual dispatch accepts meetingRef and rejects inactive meetings, unknown meetings, or non-Jitsi-capable stacks.
Dispatch creates an AgentDispatchRun linked to the meeting and passes runtime meeting credentials to job creation without exposing raw JWT in persisted status, UI, comments, or logs.
Agent Mux Job manifests include a jitsi-agent-sidecar, required JITSI_* env vars, AGENT_SOCKET_PATH, main-container socket env, and shared emptyDir volume only when meeting context is present.
Existing no-meeting dispatch and workspace PVC behavior remains unchanged.

Eight in-meeting MCP tools exist and are gated by role/capability: send_chat_message, get_meeting_transcript, get_participant_list, raise_hand, share_screen, invite_to_meeting, start_recording, react.
Meeting templates with agentConfig.autoJoin dispatch invited agent stacks when meetings start.
Web dispatch UI exposes a Meeting field only for Jitsi-capable stacks, run detail shows a meeting link, and participant lists link agent participants to dispatch runs.
Tests cover schema/controller behavior, dispatch meeting context, generated Job manifests, MCP permission denials, web dispatch/run detail behavior, and JWT non-exposure.
qualityGates: Phase 0 reuse audit is rendered before implementation work.
Dependency contracts for #624 and #620 are checked before adding duplicate Jitsi or identity abstractions.
Raw Jitsi JWT is not persisted to CRD status, comments, UI responses, logs, or PR/issue text.
Focused core and web tests pass.
securityConstraints: Keep Jitsi participant JWT runtime-only or secret-backed; persisted meetingContext must be non-secret or redacted.
Do not emit JWT values through lifecycle events, status conditions, web responses, issue comments, PR body, or logs.
Meeting MCP tools must fail closed when role/capability does not permit the action.
MeetingRef dispatch must fail closed for inactive meetings, missing providers, provider mismatch, and non-capable stacks.

repoPolicy.breakpoints: Sparse: only dependency contract ambiguity and final blocked quality gate.
repoPolicy.planOnlyArtifact: This file and the matching .mjs process definition are the only intended outputs of the planning dispatch.
